title: Mwanga District Council (Radio, Television and Telephone Transmission Tower Charges) By-laws, 2002
title_en: Mwanga District Council (Radio, Television and Telephone Transmission Tower Charges) By-laws, 2002

provisions.title: Citation and date of operation
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Mwanga District Council (Radio, Television and Telephone Transmission Tower Charges) By-laws, 2002 and shall come into operation on the date of publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These By-laws shall apply in the area of jurisdiction of Mwanga District Council only.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these By-laws unless the context requires otherwise:— “ Council ” means the Mwanga District Council; " Transmission Tower " means any Television, Radio and Telephone installation erected within the area of jurisdiction of the Council used by its owner for furthering his Television, Telephone and radio, Transmission and receiving signal. " Contractor " means any natural person or body corporate involved in the erection of any Transmission Tower for and on behalf of the owner ; “ the owner ” means any natural person or body corporate owning a Transmission Tower ; “ fees ” means such amount of money the contractor or the owner shall be required to pay to the Council before the erection of any radio, television and telephone Transmission Tower; “ charges ” means such amount of money the owner of radio television and telephone Transmission pole shall be required to pay to the Council .

provisions.title: Charges for Transmission Tower
provisions.content: (1) Every owner of Transmission Tower with a booster shall pay to the Council a charge amounting three million five hundred thousand shillings (3,500,000) only annually. (2) Every owner of a Transmission Tower without a booster shall pay to the Council a charge amounting to two million, five hundred thou­sand shillings only (2,500,000/=) annually.

provisions.title: Application for permit for erection of a Transmission Tower
provisions.content: (1) Every person intending to erect a Transmission Tower shall make application on the form to be prescribed by the Council containing such particulars as the Council may require. (2) Any permit issued by the Council for the erection of any Transmission Tower in existence at the dat of promulgation of these By-laws, shall be deemed to have been issued for a period of one year. Such permit shall not be required to comply with all provisions of these By-laws during such period of one year.

provisions.title: Special control area
provisions.content: Whenever in the opinion of the Council it is likely that any Transmission Tower permitted in terms of these By-laws may affect injuriously or disfigure any particular area within the jurisdiction of the Council , it may proclaim such area to be prohibited or restricted in any manner deemed necessary by the Council .

provisions.title: Owner responsible for maintenance
provisions.content: Every owner of a Transmission Tower shall be responsible for the maintenance of the Transmission Tower together with all supports, braces and anchors in good repair and preservation both structurally and aes­thetically.

provisions.title: Prohibition
provisions.content: Where the council is of the opinion that any site is unsuitable for the erection of a Transmission Tower the Council shall be empowered to prohibit the erection of Transmission Tower on such site.

provisions.title: Contraventions of By-laws
provisions.content: If any Transmission Tower is erected in contravention of these By-laws or of any Condition imposed under these By-laws by the Council , the Council may by written notice served to the owner of the site upon which such Transmission Tower is being erected or is situated or as the case may be on the holder of a current permission for such Transmission Tower, required him within time to be specified in such notice, remove the Transmission Tower in order that such contravention cease.

Subject to By-law 9, owner or such holder who fail to comply with the terms of such notice he shall be guilty of an offence. In addition to any penalty which may be imposed on such person for contravening these By-laws, the Council may itself carry out the terms of such notice at the expense of such owner or holder.

provisions.title: Obligation by contractor to pay fee
provisions.content: Any contractor or owner must pay to the Council a fee of Fifty thousand shillings only 50,000/= as scrutiny fee before starting to erect any Transmission Tower.

provisions.title: Validity of Permit
provisions.content: (1) A Permit issued to the owner of a Transmission Tower shall be valid until the 31 st day December of the year in which it is issued but may be renewed from year to year at the discretion of the Council . Pro­vided that the owner of Transmission Tower complies in all respect with the requirements of these By-laws. (2) The period between 1 st January and 31 st March each year is a period within which each owner of a Transmission Tower shall pay charges . (3) The owner of transmission Tower as from 31 st April of every year shall be liable to a penalty of fifty percentum of the charges and fifty percentum interest of such charges .

provisions.title: Offence
provisions.content: Every owner of a Transmission Tower who fails, refuses or neglects to pay the charges by the 31 st March of each year shall be guilty of an offence and on conviction shall be liable for a fine of shillings One hundred thousand only (100,000/=) or imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or both fine and imprisonment.
